layer_map.what: Delete the dead `ipcMain.on('ping', …)` handler + its `// IPC test` comment inside `app.whenReady().then()`; remove `ipcMain` from the line-1 electron import

key_design_decisions.why: Provably dead (research: 0 ipcRenderer/send/invoke callers, sole ipcMain handler); satisfies the constitution's "never commit debug artifacts" rule
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: Replace with a real IPC handler (§6 OOS); leave in place (violates hygiene rule)

key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: Remove only the `ipcMain` token; keep `app, shell, BrowserWindow` byte-identical
key_design_decisions.why: Minimal-change rule; removing the now-unused import is required to pass lint/typecheck (AC-5)
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: Rewrite/reorder the whole import line (unnecessary churn)

key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: Leave createWindow / app.whenReady / browser-window-created / activate / window-all-closed byte-identical
key_design_decisions.why: Behavior-preservation constraint §7; AC-2 requires lifecycle unchanged
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: Any incidental refactor of the whenReady block (out of scope)

file_impact.what_changes: Line 1: drop `ipcMain` from `import { app, shell, BrowserWindow, ipcMain } from 'electron'`. Inside `app.whenReady().then()`: delete the `// IPC test` comment and the `ipcMain.on('ping', () => console.log('pong'))` line. No other edits.

risks.risk: Edit regresses the main↔preload↔renderer process boundary
risks.mitigation: Edit confined to src/main/; no preload/renderer files touched; typecheck + lint + build gate the change; lint catches any residual unused import

risks.risk: Accidentally removing a still-needed import (`app`/`shell`/`BrowserWindow`) or altering the whenReady block, breaking app boot
risks.mitigation: Delete only the `ipcMain` token + the two ping lines; typecheck + build + smoke-launch confirm the window still opens (mirrors spec §9 risk)

dependencies: None — no packages to install, services to configure, or environment variables. Pure source deletion.
